verdict: 2026-07-19 19:34 PDT — The plan-stage architecture is otherwise sound, and the two engine-story deferrals remain settled. One source-binding contract gap remains: the new revision-plus-dirty-flag representation does not uniquely identify the audited source state.

findings.title: The snapshot binding is circular and does not identify dirty content
findings.claim: `source.revision` identifies only HEAD, while `dirty` records merely a boolean rather than the audited working-tree contents. Distinct dirty trees therefore share the same source identity. Committing the in-repo plan also necessarily advances HEAD: the smoke plan names revision `53e6a90`, while the commit containing it is `ac1d17d`, so the specified revision check would already reject the committed artifact. Separately, capturing a fresh compile-time `evaluatedAt` means recompiling an unchanged source later can change the churn window and plan, despite the stated same-state determinism invariant.
findings.alternative: Bind the plan to a deterministic fingerprint of the audited file set and contents, excluding generated plan artifacts, and have the engine recompute that fingerprint. Make the evaluation cutoff an explicit replay input or derive it deterministically from the bound source revision; alternatively, require a clean tree and keep plan artifacts outside the source revision being audited.
findings.win: Establishes an actual one-plan-to-one-source invariant, eliminates both dirty-tree ambiguity and the self-invalidating in-repo artifact cycle, and gives the future engine one unambiguous fail-closed comparison without reopening the deferred executability work.
